name: Smith Machine Calf Raise
resistance_profile: Guided bar allows heavy, balanced loading; hardest in the stretched bottom position.
cues: Keep the knees straight to bias the gastrocnemius
Full stretch at the bottom, full contraction at the top
execution_steps: Set a block or weight plates under the bar, position the bar across your upper traps, and unrack it.
Place the balls of both feet on the block with heels hanging off, standing tall with knees straight.
Lower your heels toward the floor until you feel a deep stretch in the calves.
Drive through the balls of your feet to rise as high onto your toes as possible.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower under control to the stretch.
good_when: You want to load the gastrocnemius heavily with balance taken out of the equation
You lack a dedicated standing calf-raise machine
bad_when: The Smith bar's fixed path aggravates your ankles or knees
You have no block or platform to allow a full heel drop
common_errors: Cutting the range short at the bottom
Bending the knees, which shifts load to the soleus
progressions: Add a pause in the stretched position
